wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Hidden-Gem project overview section focused on introducing the mobile application for collectors and resellers. Explain its purpose as an AI-powered authentication and marketplace platform for vintage and collectible items. Document the target audience, core value proposition, and key differentiators. Include the technology stack summary covering React Native, TypeScript, AI providers, and marketplace integrations. Provide conceptual overviews for beginners explaining the app's workflow from item discovery to monetization, while including technical details for experienced developers about the architecture and integration patterns. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases.
wiki_catalogs.dependent_files: package.json,ENVIRONMENT.md,The Relic Shop Frontend Implementation Plan.md

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend architecture documentation for Hidden-Gem's React Native application. Describe the component hierarchy starting from App.tsx as the root container, including the ErrorBoundary wrapper, QueryClientProvider for state management, and AuthProvider for authentication context. Document the navigation architecture with NavigationContainer, SafeAreaProvider, and GestureHandlerRootView integration. Explain the theme system implementation with custom dark theme configuration and color scheme management. Detail the React Query integration for data fetching and caching, including the queryClient configuration and error handling patterns. Address the component composition patterns, prop drilling prevention through context providers, and the overall frontend state management strategy.

wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Hidden-Gem introduction section focused on explaining the mobile application's purpose and value proposition. Describe how Hidden-Gem serves collectors and resellers by providing AI-powered authentication and marketplace integration for vintage and collectible items. Document the core problem the app solves, target audience personas (collectors, resellers, enthusiasts), and key benefits including item verification, market valuation, and monetization opportunities. Include the app's mission statement, vision for the collectible community, and competitive advantages over traditional authentication methods. Provide conceptual overviews for beginners explaining the complete workflow from item discovery through marketplace publication, while including technical highlights for experienced developers about the innovative approach to collectible authentication.
wiki_catalogs.dependent_files: package.json,ENVIRONMENT.md,The Relic Shop Frontend Implementation Plan.md

wiki_catalogs.name: React Native Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive React Native setup documentation covering project initialization, development environment configuration, and build processes. Document the Expo CLI workflow, TypeScript integration, and Babel configuration. Explain the application entry point structure, App.tsx root component implementation, and theme configuration. Cover development server startup, hot reloading setup, and debugging workflows. Include platform-specific considerations for iOS and Android development. Document asset management, bundle optimization, and production build processes. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and dependency conflicts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for Hidden-Gem's PostgreSQL database schema. Detail all entity relationships including core tables (users, userSettings, stashItems, articles, notifications), marketplace tables (sellers, products, listings, integrations), and auxiliary tables (conversations, messages, pushTokens, priceTracking). Document field definitions, data types, primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints for each table. Explain the purpose and relationships between tables, showing how user data connects to marketplace operations. Include database schema diagrams illustrating foreign key relationships and referential integrity. Document data validation rules, business constraints, and unique indexes. Provide examples of typical data structures for each entity and explain the rationale behind composite keys and UUID usage. Address data lifecycle considerations and cascading delete behaviors.

wiki_catalogs.name: Express Server
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Express.js server implementation. Document server initialization, middleware configuration including CORS setup, body parsing, request logging, and error handling. Explain the modular route registration system and how routes are organized. Detail the Expo manifest serving functionality for mobile app distribution. Cover the landing page template system and dynamic URL generation. Include examples of middleware setup, route registration patterns, and server configuration options. Document security considerations, logging strategies, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: eBay Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive eBay marketplace integration documentation. Document the eBay OAuth2 authentication flow including client credentials setup, refresh token management, and access token generation. Detail the complete API integration covering listing retrieval, inventory management, and CRUD operations for eBay offers and inventory items. Explain the category mapping system for automatic category assignment based on item types. Document the listing creation workflow, price updates, quantity modifications, and listing termination processes. Cover error handling strategies, rate limiting considerations, and API response parsing. Include security best practices for managing eBay API credentials, refresh token rotation, and environment configuration (sandbox vs production). Provide practical examples of API usage patterns, common integration scenarios, and troubleshooting guides for eBay-specific issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication system documentation for Hidden-Gem's user login and registration functionality. Document the Supabase integration including email/password authentication, Google OAuth integration, and session management. Explain the AuthContext provider implementation, authentication state handling, and protected route protection. Detail the authentication flow from initial login/signup through session persistence and automatic re-authentication. Cover error handling, loading states, and authentication status checking. Document the useAuth hook implementation, authentication callbacks, and session lifecycle management. Include security considerations such as password policies, account verification requirements, and session timeout handling. Provide practical examples of authentication implementation in components, protected route guards, and authentication state consumption patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Push Notifications
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive push notification documentation for Hidden-Gem's real-time alert system. Document the push notification registration process including token management for iOS, Android, and web platforms. Explain the notification delivery mechanism using Expo Push API, message formatting, and delivery status tracking. Detail the notification service implementation including token storage, message queuing, and error handling strategies. Cover the client-side notification handling with Expo Notifications, permission management, and notification display customization. Document notification types including general alerts, price changes, and marketplace updates. Include examples of push notification setup, token registration workflows, and integration patterns with the frontend application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for authentication API endpoints. Document login, logout, and session management endpoints. Cover user registration, password reset, and account verification flows. Include request/response schemas, authentication headers, token management, and security considerations. Provide examples of successful authentication flows and error handling patterns. Document Supabase integration patterns and session validation mechanisms used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Provider System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the AI provider factory system in Hidden-Gem. Detail the provider configuration interface including supported providers (Google Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, Custom), API key management, and endpoint configuration. Explain the provider selection logic, validation mechanisms, and security restrictions for custom endpoints. Document the unified analysis interface that abstracts different AI providers behind a common API. Cover provider-specific implementations including authentication methods, model selection, and response parsing. Include examples of provider configuration, connection testing, and error handling strategies. Address provider switching, fallback mechanisms, and performance considerations for each supported AI service.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for Hidden-Gem development setup. Include detailed environment setup instructions covering Node.js, Expo CLI, and platform-specific requirements for iOS and Android development. Document the installation process step-by-step, including dependency installation via npm/yarn, environment variable configuration, and initial project setup. Provide clear instructions for running the development server locally, connecting physical devices, and using the Expo Go app. Include troubleshooting common setup issues, platform-specific considerations, and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Address both local development and cloud-based development environments like Replit integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed backend services architecture documentation for Hidden-Gem's Express.js server. Describe the server initialization process in index.ts, middleware configuration, and error handling strategies. Document the modular routing system in routes.ts with clear separation of concerns for different API endpoint groups. Explain the database layer integration with Drizzle ORM, connection management, and transaction handling. Detail the AI provider service architecture including the factory pattern implementation for dynamic AI service selection and retry mechanisms. Document the eBay service integration patterns, API authentication flows, and error handling strategies. Address the service layer pattern implementation, business logic encapsulation, and inter-service communication patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Hidden-Gem technology stack section focused on documenting the complete tech architecture and development tools. Detail the frontend stack including React Native, TypeScript, Expo, React Navigation, and TanStack Query. Document the backend stack covering Express.js, Drizzle ORM, PostgreSQL, and Node.js. Explain AI integration technologies including Google Gemini and OpenAI APIs. Cover authentication systems using Supabase, marketplace integrations for eBay and WooCommerce, and notification services. Include version compatibility requirements, development dependencies, and build tools. Provide rationale for technology choices, performance considerations, and future roadmap implications. Document development environment setup requirements and IDE recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Navigation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed navigation system documentation for the React Navigation implementation. Document the hierarchical navigation structure including RootStackNavigator, MainTabNavigator, and specialized stack navigators for different app sections. Explain screen routing patterns, parameter passing between screens, and navigation state management. Cover tab navigation implementation, stack navigation behaviors, and deep linking configuration. Include practical examples of navigation actions, screen transitions, and conditional navigation flows. Document navigation ref usage, screen options configuration, and custom header implementations. Address navigation performance optimization and memory management considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: ORM Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Drizzle ORM configuration and setup in Hidden-Gem. Explain the drizzle.config.ts configuration including database connection settings, schema mapping, and migration configuration. Document the database connection initialization in server/db.ts, including connection pooling, SSL configuration, and environment variable usage. Detail the type generation process and how TypeScript types are automatically created from the schema. Explain the database client initialization, connection management, and transaction handling patterns. Document the schema import structure and how tables are organized across multiple files. Include examples of database operations, query building patterns, and type-safe database interactions. Address connection troubleshooting, performance optimization settings, and production deployment considerations for the database connection.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for all backend endpoints. Document authentication endpoints including login, logout, and session management. Cover item analysis endpoints for AI-powered authentication and valuation. Detail marketplace integration endpoints for eBay and WooCommerce including listing management, order processing, and inventory sync. Document notification endpoints for push token registration and alert management. Include endpoint specifications with HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error handling. Provide examples of API usage, parameter validation, and response formatting.

wiki_catalogs.name: WooCommerce Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed WooCommerce marketplace integration documentation. Document the REST API configuration process including store URL setup, API credentials management, and authentication methods. Explain the product publishing workflow covering product creation, variation management, and inventory synchronization with WooCommerce. Detail the order management integration including order fetching, status updates, and fulfillment coordination. Document the marketplace settings screen functionality for configuring WooCommerce connections and managing API credentials. Cover error handling for API connectivity issues, authentication failures, and data synchronization problems. Include security considerations for API key protection, HTTPS requirements, and credential storage. Provide examples of product data mapping between the application and WooCommerce, common integration patterns, and troubleshooting guides for WooCommerce-specific challenges.

wiki_catalogs.name: Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed profile management documentation for Hidden-Gem's user profile handling and personal settings. Document the profile editing interface, user information display, and profile update workflows. Explain the integration with Supabase user data, profile data synchronization, and real-time updates. Detail the settings configuration options including notification preferences, theme selection, and personal stash settings. Cover profile data validation, form handling, and error management during profile updates. Document the relationship between user profiles and marketplace integrations, including API key management and account linking. Include examples of profile data structures, update operations, and profile-related database queries. Address profile privacy settings, data export capabilities, and profile deletion procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Price Tracking Alerts
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Hidden-Gem's price tracking and alert system. Document the price tracking configuration including alert threshold settings, tracking activation/deactivation, and user preference management. Explain the automated price monitoring system including scheduled price checks, percentage change calculations, and alert triggering mechanisms. Detail the price tracking database schema including tracking records, price history, and scheduling fields. Cover the price alert notification system including increase/decrease detection, formatted message generation, and user notification delivery. Document the price tracking service implementation including scheduled job processing, error handling, and performance optimization. Include examples of enabling price tracking, configuring alert thresholds, and monitoring price change alerts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analysis Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for item analysis API endpoints. Document image upload, AI analysis triggering, and result retrieval endpoints. Cover authentication validation, pricing analysis, and SEO optimization endpoints. Include request schemas for image processing, AI provider configuration, and response formats. Provide examples of analysis workflows and error handling for failed validations. Document the retry mechanism for AI analysis with feedback integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Item Analysis Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the item analysis workflow in Hidden-Gem's AI system. Explain the complete analysis pipeline from image capture through AI processing to result generation. Document the analysis prompt structure, image preprocessing requirements, and multi-modal input handling for different AI providers. Detail the AnalysisResult interface including legacy and enhanced fields, confidence scoring, and authentication assessment criteria. Cover the retry mechanism workflow for seller feedback incorporation, including prompt templating and iterative analysis. Explain result parsing, validation, and fallback strategies for incomplete responses. Include examples of analysis requests, result interpretation, and integration with marketplace publishing workflows. Address performance optimization, rate limiting, and cost management strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for Hidden-Gem's full-stack mobile application architecture. Describe the high-level design showing the React Native frontend, Express.js backend, and PostgreSQL database layers. Document the architectural patterns including Repository pattern with Drizzle ORM, Service Layer pattern, and Factory pattern for AI providers. Explain component interactions between client-side navigation, API endpoints, database operations, and external service integrations. Include system boundaries, data flows from camera capture through AI analysis to marketplace publishing, and integration patterns with Supabase, eBay, and WooCommerce. Address technical decisions around mobile-first design, offline capabilities, and real-time features. Document technology stack choices, third-party dependencies, and version compatibility requirements.

Document the build process including production builds, asset optimization, bundle management, and platform-specific packaging for iOS and Android. Explain the environment configuration management including environment variables, database setup, and API configuration for production environments. Detail the deployment pipeline covering automated builds, testing integration, and release management. Cover the database migration process, schema updates, and data preservation strategies during deployments. Document the cloud deployment options including Expo EAS build service, hosting considerations, and scaling requirements. Include monitoring setup, error tracking, and post-deployment validation procedures. Provide examples of deployment scripts, environment configuration, and troubleshooting deployment issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines documentation for Hidden-Gem's coding standards and best practices. Document the TypeScript configuration including compiler options, type safety practices, and module resolution. Explain the ESLint configuration covering code quality rules, import sorting, and formatting standards. Detail the Prettier configuration for code formatting consistency across the team. Cover the design guidelines including component architecture patterns, naming conventions, and architectural decision records. Document the contribution workflow including development setup, branch management, pull request procedures, and code review guidelines. Include performance optimization guidelines, security best practices, and accessibility requirements. Provide examples of code formatting, architectural patterns, and development workflow implementation.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Hidden-Gem Project Overview

## Project Introduction

Hidden-Gem is a comprehensive mobile application designed for collectors, resellers, and enthusiasts to discover, analyze, and monetize vintage and collectible items. The project combines advanced AI-powered item analysis with marketplace integration capabilities, providing users with a complete workflow from item discovery to sale.

### Purpose Statement
The application serves as a bridge between collectors and the digital marketplace ecosystem, offering intelligent item analysis powered by AI and seamless integration with popular selling platforms like eBay and WooCommerce.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **AI-Powered Discovery**: Intelligent item identification and categorization through advanced image analysis
- **Multi-Platform Integration**: Seamless publishing to eBay and WooCommerce marketplaces
- **Community Knowledge**: Curated articles and guides for collectors
- **User-Friendly Experience**: Intuitive mobile interface for item scanning and management

### Target Audience
- Vintage and antique collectors
- Resellers and consignment shop owners
- Thrift store operators
- Anyone interested in appraising and selling collectible items

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern full-stack architecture with clear separation between frontend, backend, and database layers:

```mermaid
graph TD
    MobileApp[Mobile App - React Native] --> API[Express API Server]
    API --> Database[(PostgreSQL Database)]
    API --> AI[AI Provider Services]
    API --> Marketplaces[Marketplace APIs]
    
    AI --> Gemini[Google Gemini AI]
    Marketplaces --> eBay[eBay API]
    Marketplaces --> WooCommerce[WooCommerce REST API]
    
    MobileApp --> Supabase[Authentication]
    MobileApp --> Storage[Image Storage]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer (React Native)**
- Native mobile application with cross-platform support
- Modern navigation architecture with React Navigation
- Theme-based UI components with dark mode support
- Real-time data synchronization via React Query

**Backend Layer (Node.js/Express)**
- RESTful API with comprehensive marketplace integration
- Authentication and authorization services
- Image processing and AI analysis pipeline
- Background job scheduling for periodic tasks

**Data Layer**
- PostgreSQL database with Drizzle ORM
- Comprehensive schema supporting both personal stash and marketplace features
- Real-time notifications and push messaging

### Design Patterns

The application implements several key architectural patterns:

- **Repository Pattern**: Database operations abstracted through Drizzle ORM
- **Service Layer Pattern**: Business logic encapsulated in dedicated service classes
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic AI provider selection and configuration
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time notifications and state management

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Frontend Entry Point**
- `client/App.tsx` - Application root component with theme configuration and navigation setup

**Backend Entry Point**
- `server/index.ts` - Express server initialization with middleware configuration and route registration

### Core Modules

**Database Schema (`shared/schema.ts`)**
- Comprehensive PostgreSQL schema supporting user management, item tracking, marketplace integration, and AI audit trails
- Advanced indexing strategies for optimal query performance
- Foreign key relationships ensuring data integrity

**API Routes (`server/routes.ts`)**
- Modular route organization with clear separation of concerns
- Comprehensive marketplace integration endpoints
- AI analysis and retry mechanisms
- Real-time notification management

**Frontend Navigation (`client/navigation/`)**
- Hierarchical navigation structure with tab-based main navigation
- Type-safe parameter passing between screens
- Authentication-aware routing

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**
- `.env` file support for sensitive configuration
- Multi-environment deployment strategy
- Development vs production configuration differences

**Build Configuration**
- TypeScript compilation with strict typing
- ESLint and Prettier for code quality
- Automated build and deployment scripts

### External Dependencies

**Frontend Dependencies**
- React Native ecosystem for cross-platform mobile development
- React Navigation for sophisticated routing
- TanStack Query for data fetching and caching
- Supabase for authentication and real-time features

**Backend Dependencies**
- Express.js for HTTP server functionality
- Drizzle ORM for database abstraction
- Multer for file upload handling
- Google GenAI for AI-powered item analysis

### Integration Points

**AI Integration**
- Google Gemini AI for item analysis and authentication verification
- Configurable AI provider system supporting multiple providers
- Retry mechanisms for improved accuracy

**Marketplace Integration**
- eBay API integration for listing creation and management
- WooCommerce REST API for WordPress-based e-commerce
- OAuth authentication flows for secure marketplace access

**Authentication**
- Supabase authentication for user management
- Session-based authentication with automatic token refresh
- Role-based access control for different user types

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    Auth[Authentication Context] --> App[Application Root]
    App --> Navigation[Navigation Container]
    Navigation --> Screens[Screens]
    
    Screens --> QueryClient[React Query Client]
    QueryClient --> API[API Endpoints]
    API --> Database[(Database)]
    
    Screens --> Components[UI Components]
    Components --> Theme[Theme Provider]
    
    API --> AI[AI Analysis Service]
    API --> Marketplaces[Marketplace Services]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Item Discovery and Analysis**
- Camera-based item scanning with real-time preview
- AI-powered item identification and authentication
- Comprehensive market analysis and valuation
- SEO optimization for marketplace listings

**Personal Collection Management**
- Digital stash for tracking collected items
- Detailed item metadata and categorization
- Value tracking and market trend monitoring
- Publication management across multiple platforms

**Educational Content**
- Curated articles and guides for collectors
- Market insights and buying/selling strategies
- Authentication techniques and expert advice

### Implementation Highlights

**AI-Powered Analysis (`server/ai-providers.ts`)**
- Advanced item authentication with confidence scoring
- Market valuation analysis with historical comparison
- Multi-platform listing optimization
- Fallback mechanisms for robust operation

**Marketplace Integration (`server/ebay-service.ts`, `server/ai-seo.ts`)**
- Automated listing creation on eBay and WooCommerce
- Dynamic SEO optimization for maximum visibility
- Error handling and retry mechanisms
- Compliance with marketplace requirements

**Real-time Notifications (`server/services/notification.ts`)**
- Push notification system for price changes and updates
- Configurable alert thresholds and user preferences
- Cross-platform notification delivery

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Discovery
    Discovery --> Scanning: Capture Item
    Scanning --> Analysis: Process Images
    Analysis --> Valuation: AI Assessment
    Valuation --> Decision: Review Results
    
    Decision --> Publish: Ready to Sell
    Decision --> Edit: Need Changes
    Decision --> Save: Keep for Later
    
    Publish --> Listing: Create Listings
    Listing --> Monitoring: Track Performance
    Monitoring --> Alerts: Price Changes
    
    Edit --> Analysis: Re-analyze
    Save --> [*]
    Alerts --> Monitoring: Update Tracking
```

The Hidden-Gem project represents a sophisticated solution combining cutting-edge AI technology with practical marketplace functionality, providing collectors and resellers with powerful tools to maximize the value of their vintage and collectible items while maintaining a focus on user experience and reliability.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [App.tsx](client/App.tsx)
- [index.ts](server/index.ts)
- [routes.ts](server/routes.ts)
- [schema.ts](shared/schema.ts)
- [RootStackNavigator.tsx](client/navigation/RootStackNavigator.tsx)
- [HomeScreen.tsx](client/screens/HomeScreen.tsx)
- [StashScreen.tsx](client/screens/StashScreen.tsx)
- [query-client.ts](client/lib/query-client.ts)
- [AuthContext.tsx](client/contexts/AuthContext.tsx)
- [discover_flow.yml](.maestro/discover_flow.yml)
- [scan_flow.yml](.maestro/scan_flow.yml)
- [app.json](app.json)
- [drizzle.config.ts](drizzle.config.ts)
- [ai-providers.ts](server/ai-providers.ts)

</blog>
